method: Read-only live GHL API sweep across ~25 object types + full contact field-usage scan (11,491 contacts).

findings.evidence: custom_objects.consent_log exists in production with 1 field (Recorded At) and 0 records; not referenced by any workflow. Debris from the original plan's abandoned custom-object spike. (Lead Source object was never created.)
findings.recommendation: Delete the empty Consent Log object so the schema matches the no-custom-objects decision. Safe: zero records, zero dependencies.

findings.area: TCPA / consent has no single source of truth
findings.evidence: 9 consent fields on the Contact across two parallel half-built schemas (consent_source vs consent__source, consent_date / consent_time / consent__timestamp, sms_optin_date / sms_consent_updated_at / sms_double_optin / sms_optin_source). Opt-in proof populated on ~0 contacts; the proposed Consent Log fix sits empty.
findings.recommendation: Pick ONE consent schema, map the fragments into it, fix capture at the form/survey, and store DND/STOP authority in WF 11. Legal exposure at $1.5M/mo SMS, not just hygiene.

findings.evidence: Full scan of 11,491 contacts: 38 of 141 custom fields have 0 population. Of those, 7 are clearly safe (junk/duplicate/rejected), 10 are empty-but-critical (attribution gclid/fbclid, consent, EMR, engine chatbot_*) and must be kept, 21 need a per-field workflow check.
findings.recommendation: Delete the 7 safe fields (see audit/field_usage.json); protect the 10 critical; triage the 21 against the workflow build. Sequence in Phase 5 cleanup unless done sooner with sign-off.

findings.area: Governance is the root cause and is unaddressed
findings.evidence: 37 users (6 admins) with broad write access; no naming gate or creation control. This is why 6 months of workflow/field/tag sprawl accumulated.
findings.recommendation: Reduce admin count, add a 'tag vs field vs object' creation rule, and stand up the weekly data-quality report as a standing gate. Fixes the cause, not just the symptom.

findings.evidence: Only 1 custom value exists (a junk 'test=hell'). Booking URLs, phone, footer, and hours are hardcoded across 135 workflows.
findings.recommendation: Centralize shared constants as Custom Values so copy is edited once, not per-workflow. Removes a major source of duplicated/brittle message copy.

findings.evidence: 0 saved SMS/email templates (snippets). All message copy lives only inside workflows.
findings.recommendation: Preserve all copy before pausing any workflow. The message database (db/mwc.db) is the canonical external copy of every message.

findings.area: Surveys are an uncovered capture surface
findings.evidence: 4 surveys exist (Medical Intake - Onboarding, Intake EOB, Post-Appointment Feedback, Qualification Onboarding). Medical Intake is a SURVEY, not a form.
findings.recommendation: Verify 04. Medical Intake Chase stops on the survey submission (not a form), or the chase will never terminate. Fold surveys into the consolidation scope.

findings.evidence: Live counts exceed the audit snapshot: 141 fields (was 135), 318 tags (was 305), 11,491 contacts, 7,153 opportunities (was 6,775). The account is actively growing.
findings.recommendation: Re-snapshot immediately before the revenue backfill so reconciliation math uses current data.

findings.evidence: 18 trigger links, 9 funnels/websites, 2 products are not addressed by the plan. Trigger links and funnels feed attribution and checkout.
findings.recommendation: Explicitly mark out of scope, but note the attribution dependency so the lead-capture work accounts for them.

findings.evidence: The PIT token returns 401 for custom-menus and snapshots; 404/422 for some media/phone endpoints. Create+delete on custom fields IS authorized (verified).
findings.recommendation: Broaden the PIT token's scopes if fully automated execution needs custom-menus or snapshot access.
